title: Migrating Informix Overflow Tables

Migrating Informix Overflow Tables

Migrating Informix Overflow Tables

In all Informix connectors prior to Uniface V8.2.03-B306, the size of the base table part of the variable-length field is either 2, 4, 6, or 8 KB (depending on the sum of the size of the fixed-length fields), minus the sum of the size of the fixed-length fields. As of Uniface V8.2.03-B306, the size of the base table part of the variable-length field is always 8 KB minus the sum of the size of the fixed-length fields. This results in an incompatibility.

The table size is stored in the table definition in the database and defined during table creation. In Uniface, tables are either created on the fly, created by SQL scripts generated with the Uniface Create Table facility, or, in the case of Uniface Repository tables, by SQL scripts that are available in the /sql subdirectory of your Uniface installation.

CAUTION: To use the Informix drivers of Uniface V8.2.03-B306 or later, the tables in the Informix databasemustbe created with the SQL (scripts) created by Uniface V8.2.03-B306 or later. Data in tables created with old SQL scripts can be corrupted during storage if accessed by Uniface V8.2.03-B306 or later. No error messages are returned by the database, by the database connector, or by Uniface.

If your application model contains fields that use overflow tables, perform the following steps to migrate the data in your Informix database:

  1. Make a backup of your Informix database.
  2. Using Uniface V8.2.03-B305 or older, analyze your models that contain entities with fields that use overflow tables.
  3. Using Uniface V8.2.03-B305 or older, export all data belonging to these entities to TRX files. You can use the Convert Data facility or the /cpy command line switch.
    Note: Do not use a database export utility. You must use Uniface to export, because only Uniface recognizes the overflow tables.
  4. Use SQL to drop all involved tables.
  5. Install Uniface V8.2.03-B306 or later, and configure Uniface to use your Informix database. No additional (assignment) settings are required.
  6. Using Uniface V8.2.03-B306 or later, import all your data using the /cpy command line switch.
Was this topic helpful?
